name: Boardera
description: Boardera Software is focused on helping PCB manufacturers and EMS assemblers bring their services online with cutting edge e-commerce tooling built specifically for the PCB industry. Using smart design and automation results in drastic reductions in overhead, costs and lead times. Since August 2022, Boardera’s services are also available via Siemens’ PCB Flow platform. Collaboration with Siemens: Ecosystem Partners. Dynamo focus areas: Supply Chain & Logistics. name: Cybellum
description: Cybellum's Product Security Platform and services enables device manufacturers to manage the main aspects of their cybersecurity operations across business units and lifecycle stages. From SBOM to Vulnerability Management, Compliance Validation, and Incident Response, teams ensure their connected products are fundamentally secure and compliant – and stay that way. Cybellum’s combined offering with Siemens’ Polarion Application Lifecycle Management solution (ALM) enables users to manage cybersecurity and safety threats throughout the engineering cycle. Cybellum was acquired by LG Electronics in 2021. Collaboration with Siemens: Ecosystem Partners. Dynamo focus areas: Digital Twin & Simulation. name: Cybord

Cybord’s visual AI analytics ensures the quality, authenticity, and traceability of 100% of electronic components. It reduces scrap, failure rates and waste, and ultimately increases customer profits. Cybord’s solution is part of Siemens’ offering in the electronics market. name: Inspekto
description: Inspekto enables out-of-the-box visual quality inspection and requires no expertise in vision solutions or AI. Inspekto is easy to deploy and use, scalable, and provides the necessary connectivity to existing automation systems. Inspekto joined the Siemens Dynamo program in February 2020, and was acquired by Siemens Digital Industries in February 2024. During this period, it was deployed in different Siemens factories and has been building integration with Siemens Industrial Edge. See also this Inspekto presentation at SPS 2024 . Collaboration with Siemens: Investment Opportunities. Dynamo focus areas: Artificial Intelligence & Computer Vision. From their website: Busting myths on AI-based visual inspection | Siemens Skip to main content Busting myths on AI-based visual inspection Rejects disrupt production and increase costs, while defective parts can harm brand reputation. Manufacturers are adopting AI-based visual inspection, such as Siemens’ Inspekto. Athina Liatsa, Global Marketing Manager for Industrial Apps, addresses five key myths. Myth 1: AI inspection is difficult to install Inspekto is delivered with everything you need – it contains a Siemens Industrial PC, a camera, a light, and all the required cables. That means there are no extra orders to place, no looking around to see if you have the right connectors. Where to install it? That’s totally open too, because the solution has been conceived for a variety of use cases – from production lines to robots. Multi-camera setups are also possible, for example in complex production cells where several inspections are done at the same time. Inspekto can be fully integrated with PLC or MES/ERP systems, which makes it great for the inspection of incoming goods or the final quality inspection of finished products. Once you have decided on the use case and the hardware is in place, AI guide

name: Instrumental
description: Instrumental has built the leading Manufacturing AI and Data Platform for brands and manufacturers developing and producing consumer and mission-critical electronics. The platform optimizes assembly, operations, and quality control processes to accelerate time-to-market, improve yields, eliminate rework, and save engineering time. When combined with Teamcenter Quality, Instrumental supports Siemens’ closed loop quality approach. This helps electronics OEMs go from Quality Management to Quality Optimization. Instrumental's solutions can be purchased on Siemens Digital Exchange . Collaboration with Siemens: Ecosystem Partners. Dynamo focus areas: Artificial Intelligence & Computer Vision.

name: ioTech
description: ioTech from Jerusalem has developed a multi-material additive manufacturing technology that can process almost any industry certified material at high resolution and high speed. In 2019 we introduced ioTech to our innovation partner ASM Assembly Systems GmbH & Co. KG. This led to a successful technology collaboration as well as capital investment . Guenter Schindler, COO of ASM SMT Solutions talks here about ASM SMT's collaboration with Siemens Dynamo . Collaboration with Siemens: Investment Opportunities.

name: Percepto
description: Percepto is revolutionizing the way vital infrastructure and assets are inspected and monitored by providing a holistic fully automated solution. Siemen Energy is using Percepto to monitor power plants since 2022. Collaboration with Siemens: Startups as Suppliers. Dynamo focus areas: Robotics & Automation.

name: Portcast
description: Portcast , Headquartered in Singapore, provides predictive visibility and demand forecasting for international transportation. Leveraging proprietary machine learning and generative AI, the platform processes over 5 million data points daily from 370+ ocean carriers and airlines, 6,000+ vessels, 1,200 ports, direct integrations with 45+ terminals. Siemens Digital logistics partnered with Portcast in 2023 to enable more resilient and transparent supply chains for shippers and freight forwarders. Portcast is also available in the Siemens Xcelerator Marketplace . Collaboration with Siemens: Portfolio Partners. Dynamo focus areas: Supply Chain & Logistics. From their website: Portcast | Supply Chain Visibility Software | Real-Time Supply Chain Visibility Supply Chain Visibility Software Predict Shipment Delays. Reduce Freight Costs. Portcast connects your shipment data to your orders, contracts, and invoices. Purpose-built AI surfaces risks before they escalate and recommends the right actions to protect your service levels and margins. Book a demo Chosen by these teams and many more The Freight Intelligence Platform for Global Transportation Teams. name: Razor Labs
description: Razor Labs (TASE: RZR) a leading developer of AI solutions for industrial manufacturing processes, in March 2022 it has announced that it was selected by Siemens Energy to implement a pilot at its O&M site in Israel , for the station’s cooling system. DataMind AI predicts the future power supply of the station, verifies the optimality of the cooling system performance and the optimum output of the station. name: Realtime Robotics
description: Realtime Robotics provides a powerful suite of software tools for programming robotic arms and the systems they are used in. They automate the programming and execution of robot motions, cutting out difficult and costly aspects of operating robots. Visualize, prioritize and simulate path plans to validate robot programs with a seamless Process Simulate plug-in . Collaboration with Siemens: Portfolio Partners. Dynamo focus areas: Robotics & Automation. name: Retrocausal
description: Retrocausal is an industry leader in AI-powered intelligence augmentation systems that helps manufacturing workers avoid assembly mistakes, be more efficient at their daily jobs, and improve the processes they drive. Its Assembly Copilot™ and Kaizen Copilot™ products use machine learning, computer vision and AI to continuously guide, track and analyze assembly processes. Retrocausal joined the Siemens Frontier program in 2021, and developed integration with Siemens Process Simulate Human as part of a funded research project. The next step was to interface Siemens' Opcenter Execution platform, in order to streamline the production and manage quality more effectively. Collaboration with Siemens: Research Partners. Dynamo focus areas: Artificial Intelligence & Computer Vision. name: SkillReal
description: SkillReal is realizing its mission to deliver accurate & reliable next-generation Digital Twin technologies across industries, helping global manufacturers to significantly shorten time of serial production, and improve productivity and quality. Through its partnership with Siemens, SkillReal was able to secure funding from the Bird Foundation – a US-Israel fund investing in joint technology research projects. Since 2023, SkillReal is also a portfolio partner of Siemens . Collaboration with Siemens: Portfolio Partners, Research Partners. Dynamo focus areas: AR/VR & Industrial Metaverse, Digital Twin & Simulation.
